description_long: This bungalow features a low-pitched, expansive hip roof with deep overhangs, providing ample shade to the structure below. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely stucco or painted concrete, and the building sits on a rectangular plot of green lawn. The front facade includes a small covered porch with steps leading up to the main entrance, flanked by large windows that allow natural light into the living spaces. The windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with additional windows on the sides and rear for cross-ventilation. The roof is clad in dark shingles, and the overall design is compact and single-storied, typical of suburban residential neighborhoods. The structure appears solid and permanent, with clear architectural lines and functional outdoor access.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide hip roof, light-colored walls, and a small covered front porch, surrounded by a green lawn. Large windows and a symmetrical facade give it a modern, suburban appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, and two large front windows.
description_view_1: Rear view with smaller windows and no porch, highlighting the back facade and roof overhang.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the extended hip roof and two side windows, with the porch partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a single large window and the depth of the roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with additional side and rear windows
